hooks.prompt:
Before ending this session, do two things:

1. KNOWLEDGE CAPTURE: Evaluate if specialized project knowledge was discovered during this work that should be captured as scoped .claude/rules/ files. Worth capturing: complex multi-component interactions, error recovery procedures, deep system patterns, framework/library quirks, patterns that would save significant time if known upfront. NOT worth capturing: one-off fixes, knowledge already in existing rules, generic programming knowledge. If significant knowledge was discovered, invoke the /knowledge-capture skill to package it.

2. COMMIT MEMORY FILES: After knowledge capture (or if skipped), check git status for changes to any of these memory-related paths:
   - .claude/rules/*.md (rule files)
   - .claude/rules/.optimization-log.md (optimization log)
   If any were created or modified during this session (by /rule-manager, /knowledge-capture, or the SessionStart hook), commit ONLY those files with message: 'chore(memory): update rules and optimization log'. Do NOT include non-memory files in this commit.

If neither action is needed, proceed with stopping.
Verify task completion: check acceptance criteria, ensure tests pass, and update task status in the activity log.
